name: spglib (Crystal Space Group Analysis)

performance.notes: C extension; very fast for typical crystal structures

agent_readiness.notes: Crystal symmetry analysis and space group determination. import spglib. cell = (lattice, positions, numbers). dataset = spglib.get_symmetry_dataset(cell, symprec=1e-5). dataset['international']. dataset['number']. dataset['pointgroup']. dataset['wyckoffs']. std_cell = spglib.standardize_cell(cell). primitive = spglib.find_primitive(cell). symmetry = spglib.get_symmetry(cell). Symmetry operations, rotation matrices. Used by pymatgen, ASE, phonopy. Magnetic spglib: get_magnetic_symmetry_dataset(). BZ paths: seekpath integration.

gotchas: symprec (tolerance) parameter critically affects space group detection
Input cell must be (lattice, positions, atomic_numbers) tuple — not ASE Atoms directly
Some space group operations require careful handling of magnetic symmetry
best_when: Crystal space group determination and symmetry analysis
Identifying Wyckoff positions and symmetry operations
Used as dependency in pymatgen, phonopy, and ASE workflows
avoid_when: When pymatgen's higher-level interface is sufficient
summary: spglib determines crystal space groups and symmetry operations. get_symmetry_dataset((lattice, positions, numbers)). dataset['international'], ['number'], ['wyckoffs']. standardize_cell(). find_primitive(). BSD-3 by Atsushi Togo. C extension, very fast. Essential dependency for pymatgen, phonopy, ASE. symprec parameter controls detection tolerance. Best for crystal symmetry analysis in computational materials science.
